title: Time-aware CatBoost with native categorical combinations
summary: CatBoost’s ordered statistics and `has_time` mode are designed to avoid target leakage in high-cardinality categoricals, which fits `Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Year`, and their interactions better than manual frequency features alone.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered statistics and `has_time` mode are designed to avoid target leakage in high-cardinality categoricals, which fits `Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Year`, and their interactions better than manual frequency features alone.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoost on raw categorical columns plus a few crossed strings such as `Driver__Race`, `Driver__Compound`, `Race__Compound`, `Race__Year__Compound`; sort rows by Race-Year, Driver, LapNumber and enable `has_time=True` so ordered CTRs only use prior rows.
expected_effect: Can capture driver/race/compound priors and interaction effects more cleanly than hand-built encodings, with less overfit on rare drivers.
risk: If row order is wrong, ordered CTRs become noisy or leaky; compare against current baseline under race-group holdout, not random CV.
